title: Lynia Delline
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image= |name=Lynia Delline |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Near-Human|Half-Human]]''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=She/her |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Lavender]] |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings=A [[Lynia Delline's brother|brother]] |children= |affiliation=[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I have certain…abilities…with ideas. It's hard to explain. My ancestors were a little…different.|Lynia Delline|}} '''Lynia Delline''' was a [[Near-Human|half-Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who worked as an [[Aide/Legends|aide]] to [[Chief of State (Alliance)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] during the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. A woman who had inherited some unusual abilities from her ancestors, she described her work as observing things for the Chief of State. After attending one of Mothma's rallies on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Agamar/Legends|Agamar]], she met a young [[Agamarian]] named [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]], whom she helped join the Rebel Alliance. Delline followed Farlander's career as a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] closely, and the two eventually began a relationship. ==Biography== ===Alliance service=== {{Quote|There's no other task you think you could perform for the Alliance? Nothing other than flying one of those death traps?''"
"''You wouldn't understand.''"
"''Wouldn't I? If you're still around in a few weeks, come ask me about my brother.|Lynia Delline and Keyan Farlander|}} Lynia Delline was born in the years surrounding the fall of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and grew up during the reign of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. She had a [[Lynia Delline's brother|brother]] who became a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and was either injured or killed while flying. However, like much of her early life, Delline rarely spoke of what happened. She joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] at some point before the [[Battle of Turkana/Legends|Battle of Turkana]] and became an [[Aide/Legends|aide]] to the Alliance's [[Chief of State (Alliance)/Legends|Chief of State]], [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]]. She would later describe her job as "observing things" for the Rebel leader. [[File:MonMothma-RotJAVA.png|thumb|right|200px|Delline served as an aide to Rebel Chief of State Mon Mothma during the Galactic Civil War.]] In [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]],''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' begins after the [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]] entered service with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], which ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' places in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]. ''X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide'' establishes that the [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|capture of Leia Organa]] occurs at the end of [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']]{{'s}} second tour of duty. Organa's capture takes place on 35:3:3 under the [[Great ReSynchronization]] dating system, placing it at the beginning of [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] according to [[Template:YearMathGRS|the following explanation]]. Events in ''X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide'' before the end of the second tour of duty therefore take place in 1 BBY. Delline accompanied Mothma to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Agamar/Legends|Agamar]] aboard the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']]. On Agamar, Mothma addressed members of the [[Agamar Resistance]], hoping to persuade them to join the Rebel Alliance. After the Chief of State gave her speech, Delline moved among the assembled crowd, distributing copies of the Rebel pamphlet ''[[A Call to Reason]]'' before assisting the other Rebels present with removing all evidence that the meeting had occurred. One of the [[Agamarian]]s, [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]], approached her and announced that he had decided to join the Alliance as a starfighter pilot, and Delline made sure he understood the commitment he was making before introducing him to a recruiter. Delline flew back to the ''Independence'' aboard the same [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] as Farlander and, worried that the young Rebel would suffer the same fate as her brother, attempted to persuade him to serve the Alliance in a less dangerous way. Farlander, however, was determined to join the [[Alliance Starfighter Corps/Legends|Alliance Starfighter Corps]], and she respected his decision, wishing him luck before leaving him in the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] of the ''Independence''. ===Reuniting with Farlander=== {{Quote|I thought of Lynia. Where was she right now?|Keyan Farlander, after observing Luke Skywalker and Leia Organa Solo prior to the Battle of Yavin|}} When Mon Mothma left the ''Independence'' soon after, Delline accompanied her—only returning to the Alliance [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] toward the end of [[Operation Strike Fear]], a major [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] offensive against the Rebellion. The day Delline arrived, she learned that Farlander was due to be promoted to [[Captain/Legends|captain]] and attended a small promotion ceremony conducted by [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Lagrane]]. After the ceremony, Farlander approached her and the two spoke briefly before Farlander left to attend a party being held by [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]] in his honor. Delline declined an invitation to join them, saying she had to get back to work, but gave Farlander a kiss on the cheek when she saw his disappointment. The two were quickly becoming friends and saw each other twice more before Delline's duties with Mothma took her off the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] again. [[File:FarlanderYavin-ANH.jpg|thumb|left|210px|Farlander's Y-wing in the Battle of Yavin]] She returned to the ''Independence'' again while the Alliance searched for information on a [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|new Imperial weapon]]. There, she attended an [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] meeting with Mothma, [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]], [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]], and General [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]], during which the group planned an [[Battle of Orron III|operation]] to steal some Imperial [[communications satellite]]s and use them to eavesdrop on Imperial transmissions in the [[Cron Drift/Legends|Cron Drift]]. Farlander was waiting for Delline as she left the meeting, and the two conversed with Mothma before dining together in the ship's executive dining room. Delline's duties took her off the ''Independence'' the next day but, after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], she promised to spend more time with Farlander. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Mon Mothma seems quite interested in you, Keyan.''"
"''Don't worry, Lynia. She's not my type.|Lynia Delline and Keyan Farlander|}} Delline was a [[Near-Human|half-Human]] woman whom Farlander viewed as attractive, described as having a "dazzling smile," and had [[Color/Legends|lavender]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] that seemed to change color from different angles yet conveyed a serious look. She was a private individual who spoke little of her early life and what had happened to her brother. She did describe her ancestors as a little different, which resulted in her having some unusual abilities. After meeting Farlander, she kept their meetings short and would not let them get too personal, even though there was a mutual attraction between them and she followed his career with interest when away from the ''Independence''. Though she was generally unflappable, her professional shell could be broken, such as when she blushed after Mon Mothma once commented on her attraction to Farlander in front of the pilot. Delline was loyal to the Rebellion and greatly respected Mothma's opinion. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|I could hold my own against five-to-one odds in a starfighter; you would think that I'd have better defenses against one female with heavenly eyes.|Keyan Farlander|}} Lynia Delline was created by [[Rusel DeMaria]] and first appeared in ''[[The Farlander Papers]]'', a novella that accompanied the [[1993]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']], and introduced the player character Keyan Farlander. Delline was unnamed in ''The Farlander Papers''{{StoryCite|story=The Farlander Papers|book=Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)}} but reappeared in the narrative sections of DeMaria's ''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'', which expanded on the events of the previous book and in which she served as Farlander's love interest. ==Appearances== *{{StoryCite|story=The Farlander Papers|book=Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' {{1stID|Lynia Delline}} *''[[X-Wing Collector's CD-ROM: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Delline, Lynia}} [[Category:Alliance to Restore the Republic members]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Individuals of unidentified species]] [[Category:Individuals with she/her pronouns]] [[Category:Near-Human Hybrids]]
